title: podman stats are broken after a container restarted
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When constantly polling the container statistics via a UNIX socket, after restarting the container cpu usage stats look broken and reports a CPU usage of for example  `8373583175.995965`.


<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  `curl -X GET -s -g --no-buffer --unix-socket /var/run/podman/podman.sock http://localhost/v1.12/libpod/containers/0678d763c92b5b6a62061ef2/stats  | jq '.cpu_stats'`

2. podman restart $containerid

**Describe the results you received:**

```
{
  "cpu_usage": {
    "total_usage": 36716000,
    "usage_in_kernelmode": 19350,
    "usage_in_usermode": 36696650
  },
  "system_cpu_usage": 188755861000,
  "cpu": 8373583175.995965,
  "throttling_data": {
    "periods": 0,
    "throttled_periods": 0,
    "throttled_time": 0
  }
}
```

This happens at random and it sometimes returns to normal `  "cpu": 0.007009992418851287,`

**Describe the results you expected:**

`  "cpu": 0.007009992418851287,`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Mon Aug 30 20:46:36 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

qemu/kvm virtual machine

comments.body:
I guess this happens because once it is restarted the container runs in a different cgroup so all its stats are resetted.

We'd probably need to detect when the newer usage stats are lower than the old usage stats and skip the step
@giuseppe could you take a look into fixing this?
